content: We can borrow the pedagogical vocabulary learning logic of human reflexion (reflection) in high school English classrooms to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms: just as human learners solidify correct word usage by repeatedly reflecting on mismatches between their output and standard dictionary definitions, LLMs can refine their reasoning by reflecting on mismatches between their trial outputs and grounded, verifiable ground-truth specifications. This frames LLM self-reflection not as a generic error-checking step, but as a structured vocabulary-like acquisition process for correct problem-solving schemas.
